question: On a Camunda Forms select element, the **Default value** property is available only when which condition is met?

a.text: Searchable lets the user search entries via keyboard but is independent of whether a Default value can be configured.

b.text: The docs explicitly note that Default value is only available for static options sources, because the option set must be known at design time to bind a default.

c.text: Required only enforces that the user must pick a value at submission; it does not unlock the Default value property.

d.text: Selects can be bound to any datatype (strings are recommended); the binding type does not control whether Default value is editable.

explanation: On a select element, Default value can only be pre-configured when the Options source is static, because Camunda must know the option set at design time to bind a default. When the Options source is dynamic (resolved at runtime from an expression), the docs state Default value is not available.
